title: GitHub Copilot is good now. Here's why that matters
post:
I owe GitHub Copilot an apology.

Six months ago, I called it overrated. The suggestions were generic, the context awareness was lacking, and it often interrupted my flow more than it helped.

But after the latest updates? It's genuinely impressive now. The code completion accuracy is night-and-day better, and the new conversation mode actually understands my codebase.

If you dismissed it before like I did — give it another shot.

Are you still using Copilot or did you switch to something else? Thoughts?
